senses.definition: showing a proud, unpleasant attitude toward others and having an exaggerated sense of self-importance

examples.x: Despite his lack of experience, he acted in an arrogant manner, believing he knew better than everyone else.
Being confident is good, but don’t let it turn into being arrogant.
Her arrogant demeanor alienated her from her peers, leaving her with few friends.
She came across as arrogant when she dismissed their suggestions.
The company's CEO was known for his arrogant behavior, which created a toxic work environment.

senses.definition: brave and confident, with the ability to take risks

examples.x: The bold entrepreneur invested all of her savings into her startup, believing in its potential.
As a bold leader, he fearlessly confronts difficult decisions and challenges head-on.
The bold student volunteered to present the group's project to the entire class.
The bold entrepreneur pursued her dreams with unwavering determination, despite the odds.

senses.definition: showing concern for the well-being of others and being kind and supportive in one's actions and interactions

examples.x: She is known for her caring nature, always willing to lend a helping hand to those in need.
The caring nurse stayed by the patient's bedside throughout the night, ensuring their comfort.
He showed his caring side by volunteering at the local soup kitchen every weekend.
The teacher's caring attitude made students feel comfortable approaching her with their problems.

senses.definition: treating others with respect and honesty

examples.x: The decent neighbor offers a helping hand to those in need and maintains a friendly and respectful demeanor.
The decent boss values open communication and fairness, creating a positive and respectful work environment.
The decent student follows academic integrity principles, never cheating or plagiarizing in their coursework.
Her decent nature extends to all living beings, as she advocates for animal welfare and environmental conservation.

senses.definition: not truthful or trustworthy, often engaging in immoral behavior

examples.x: He was caught being dishonest about his involvement in the incident.
The salesman's dishonest tactics led to distrust among customers.
The company suffered financial losses due to the dishonest actions of its executives.
Cheating on exams is a dishonest practice that undermines academic integrity.
She felt betrayed by her friend's dishonest behavior, which included spreading rumors behind her back.

senses.definition: calm and not easily worried or annoyed

examples.x: She’s very easy-going and never gets stressed over small problems.
His easy-going nature makes him a great person to travel with.
Despite the chaos at work, he remained easy-going and calm.
Our boss is easy-going as long as we meet our deadlines.
He’s so easy-going that even when plans change, he just goes with the flow.

senses.definition: active and full of energy

examples.x: Mary's energetic dance moves lit up the stage during the performance.
Tim's energetic puppy eagerly bounded around the backyard, chasing after his favorite toys.
Sarah's energetic toddler kept her on her toes as he explored the playground with endless curiosity.
Jack's energetic personality made him the life of the party, always ready to engage in lively conversation and activities.
David's energetic performance on the soccer field impressed scouts and earned him a spot on the varsity team.

senses.definition: having or showing intense excitement, eagerness, or passion for something

examples.x: His enthusiastic support for the project helped drive it to success.
As an enthusiastic participant in the workshop, she asked many insightful questions.
The team's enthusiastic response to the new strategy boosted morale.
Her enthusiastic applause showed her genuine appreciation for the performance.
The enthusiastic fans cheered loudly for their favorite band.

senses.definition: having a lot of energy

examples.x: With his dynamic personality, he brings enthusiasm and energy to every situation.
Her dynamic presence on stage captivates audiences, drawing them into her performance.
Despite his age, he maintains a dynamic lifestyle, always seeking new adventures.
The dynamic leader inspires her team with her boundless energy and passion.
The dynamic atmosphere at the concert energized the crowd, creating an unforgettable experience.

senses.definition: likely to forget things or having difficulty to remember events

examples.x: She’s been quite forgetful lately, misplacing her keys all the time.
My forgetful nature led me to miss the meeting yesterday.
He’s forgetful and often forgets people’s birthdays.
I had to write everything down because I was feeling unusually forgetful.
Being forgetful, she often leaves her phone at home.

senses.definition: having an excessive and intense desire for something, especially wealth, possessions, or power

examples.x: The greedy businessman exploited his employees for profit, caring only about increasing his own wealth.
The greedy child hoarded all the candy for himself, refusing to share with his friends.
The greedy landlord raised the rent prices without improving the living conditions for tenants.
Greedy corporations prioritize profits over environmental conservation, leading to ecological destruction.
The greedy politician accepted bribes in exchange for favorable legislation, betraying the public's trust.

senses.definition: lacking warmth or friendliness

examples.x: The icy silence between them suggested something was wrong.
She gave me an icy response when I asked about her day.
Their relationship remained icy even after the conflict was resolved.
The receptionist's icy attitude made me feel unwelcome.

senses.definition: unable to wait calmly for something or someone, often feeling irritated or frustrated

examples.x: The impatient driver honked their horn repeatedly in traffic.
The customer became impatient, demanding faster service from the staff.
The kids became impatient after waiting hours for their turn.
Impatient with the delays, she decided to call customer service.
He’s always impatient when it comes to slow internet connections.

senses.definition: (of a person) very energetic and outgoing

examples.x: She is always lively, bringing energy and excitement to any gathering.
The lively child ran around the playground, laughing and playing with friends.
He's known for his lively personality, always the center of attention at social events.
Despite her age, she remains lively and active, participating in various hobbies and sports.

senses.definition: based on clear reasoning or sound judgment

examples.x: His argument was clear and logical, making it easy for everyone to understand and agree with his point of view.
She approached the problem with a logical mindset, systematically analyzing each potential solution.
It's logical to assume that if you practice regularly, your skills will improve over time.
The logical conclusion, given the evidence presented, was that the project would succeed.
They made a logical decision based on the data, avoiding emotional bias in their choice.

senses.definition: not boasting about one's abilities, achievements, or belongings

examples.x: Despite her remarkable talent, she remains modest and never seeks attention or praise.
His modest demeanor makes him approachable and easy to relate to, despite his success.
The modest scientist downplayed her groundbreaking research, attributing it to collaboration and teamwork.
He has always been modest about his skills, never boasting or seeking the spotlight.
He gave a modest reply when asked about his success.

senses.definition: experiencing frequent changes in mood, often without apparent reason or explanation

examples.x: She's so moody lately, swinging between happiness and sadness from one moment to the next.
The moody teenager withdrew into their room, refusing to talk to anyone.
He became moody whenever he was stressed at work, snapping at his coworkers for no reason.
His moody disposition made it difficult for others to predict how he would react in any given situation.

senses.definition: following the principles of wrong and right and behaving based on the ethical standards of a society

examples.x: The moral leader always prioritized honesty and fairness in decision-making, setting a positive example for others to follow.
The moral teacher instilled values of empathy and compassion in her students, fostering a sense of social responsibility.
Even in difficult situations, the moral doctor upheld patient confidentiality and acted in the best interest of their health.
The moral businessman refused to cut corners or compromise on quality, prioritizing integrity over profit.
Despite peer pressure, the moral teenager stood firm in their principles and refused to participate in harmful activities.

senses.definition: showing too much interest in people's lives, especially when it is not one's concern

examples.x: My neighbor is extremely nosy and always asks personal questions.
She was being nosy, peeking into her colleague’s email.
He’s nosy and constantly eavesdrops on other people’s conversations.
The nosy reporter asked about details that weren’t her business.
I told him to stop being nosy and respect my privacy.

senses.definition: having a hopeful and positive outlook on life, expecting good things to happen

examples.x: Despite setbacks, she remained optimistic about her future career prospects.
His optimistic attitude lifted the spirits of everyone around him during difficult times.
Despite the setbacks, he stayed optimistic that things would improve.
Even in the face of adversity, he maintained an optimistic outlook, focusing on potential solutions rather than dwelling on problems.
Optimistic investors continued to pour money into the startup despite the risks.

senses.definition: having or showing a negative view of the future and always waiting for something bad to happen

examples.x: Despite their hard work, he remained pessimistic about the chances of success.
Her pessimistic attitude towards relationships made it difficult for her to trust others.
He had a pessimistic view of the economy, believing that a recession was imminent.
The pessimistic outlook of the team members dampened morale and productivity.
The pessimistic tone of his writing reflected the author's bleak perspective on life.

senses.definition: showing or having enthusiasm or strong emotions about something one care deeply about

examples.x: She is a passionate advocate for environmental conservation and volunteers regularly for cleanup projects.
He delivered a passionate speech about social justice, inspiring others to take action.
The passionate chef poured her heart into creating innovative dishes that delighted diners.
They had a passionate debate about the future of renewable energy in their community.
Her passionate love for literature led her to pursue a career as an English teacher.

senses.definition: (of a person) realistic in facing different matters or problems

examples.x: She’s very practical and always finds solutions that work in real life.
His practical approach helped us save time and money on the project.
A practical person, she quickly came up with a plan to fix the issue.
He’s known for being practical and down-to-earth in his decisions.

senses.definition: (of a person) showing good judgment and acting by reason

examples.x: A reasonable man would consider all sides before deciding.
She is a reasonable person who listens carefully to others.
He handled the conflict like a reasonable individual.
A reasonable judge weighs evidence without bias.
They sought advice from a reasonable and experienced friend.

senses.definition: considered to be good, correct, or acceptable by the society

examples.x: He landed a respectable job in a well-known firm, which was considered a commendable achievement by his peers.
The restaurant earned a respectable rating from critics for its high-quality food and excellent service.
Her academic performance was deemed respectable by the school, earning her a place on the honor roll.
The neighborhood was known for its respectable homes and well-maintained gardens, reflecting the community's pride.
His behavior at the event was considered respectable, adhering to all the social norms and expectations of formal gatherings.

senses.definition: (of a person) having trust in one's abilities and qualities

examples.x: Despite criticism, he remained self-confident in his creative vision, believing in the value of his work.
She's self-confident, showing a positive and assured attitude in various situations.
Even in difficult situations, he remained self-confident.
She walked into the room with a self-confident smile.
The self-confident leader inspired trust and respect among team members with her clear direction.

senses.definition: capable of understanding other people's emotions and caring for them

examples.x: She has a sensitive nature, always attuned to the feelings of those around her.
He gave a sensitive response to her concerns, making her feel heard.
She has a sensitive approach when dealing with emotionally charged situations.
Being sensitive to cultural differences allows for better communication and mutual respect.
The nurse’s sensitive care helped put the patient at ease.

senses.definition: (of statements, feelings, beliefs, or behavior) showing what is true and honest, based on one's real opinions or feelings

examples.x: Her sincere apology made me feel better about the misunderstanding.
He gave a sincere speech, expressing his gratitude to everyone.
I appreciated her sincere feedback on my work.
His sincere efforts to improve were noticeable to everyone.
It was clear from his sincere tone that he truly cared about the issue.

senses.definition: very determined in one's beliefs or decisions, often showing firmness of character and persistence in achieving what one wants

examples.x: Despite facing numerous challenges, she remained strong-willed and determined to succeed in her career.
His strong-willed nature helped him overcome adversity and achieve his lifelong dream of becoming a pilot.
The strong-willed activist fought tirelessly for equal rights and social justice in her community.
They admire her strong-willed approach to parenting, balancing discipline with compassion.
In negotiations, his strong-willed stance ensured that the team's interests were protected and respected.

senses.definition: showing care and understanding toward other people, especially when they are not feeling good

examples.x: The teacher was sympathetic to the student who was struggling with the coursework.
She was very sympathetic when I told her about my difficulties at work.
The doctor was sympathetic to the patient's concerns about the upcoming surgery.
Despite their differences, they were always sympathetic to each other's feelings.
The therapist provided a sympathetic environment for her clients to share their emotions.

senses.definition: not able to be depended on or trusted to perform consistently or fulfill obligations

examples.x: He's so unreliable; he always promises to help but never shows up.
The unreliable car broke down again, leaving them stranded on the side of the road.
His unreliable memory caused him to forget important appointments.
She found the weather forecast to be unreliable, often inaccurate.
He's an unreliable friend; you can't count on him to keep his promises or be there when you need him.

senses.definition: displaying unpredictable and sudden changes in emotions and behavior

examples.x: His relationships were strained due to his unpredictable and unstable behavior.
She struggled with an unstable temperament, often alternating between extreme joy and deep despair.
Living with an unstable roommate caused tension in the household, as his mood swings could be intense and sudden.
His career suffered setbacks because of his reputation for being unstable, making colleagues hesitant to collaborate with him.

senses.definition: not confident enough in oneself, especially in one's abilities

examples.x: He felt unsure about taking on such a large responsibility at work.
I was unsure of how to respond, so I stayed silent.
She looked unsure when asked to lead the team on her own.
His unsure expression showed that he needed more training.

senses.definition: (of a person) inflexible and demanding that rules are followed precisely

examples.x: The strict teacher enforced a no-talking rule during tests.
He grew up with strict parents who had high expectations for his behavior.
The strict coach required players to adhere to a rigorous training schedule.
The strict supervisor closely monitored employees' work hours and productivity.
Despite her strict demeanor, she was fair and consistent in her enforcement of rules.

senses.definition: involving or caused by force that may result in physical damage

examples.x: The storm was so violent that it knocked down several trees.
There was a violent shaking when the earthquake hit.
The car crash was so violent that both vehicles were completely wrecked.
A violent explosion caused damage to nearby buildings.
The ship was caught in violent waves that tossed it from side to side.

senses.definition: (of a person)  tolerant, flexible, or relaxed in enforcing rules or standards, often forgiving and understanding toward others

examples.x: The lenient teacher allowed students extra time to complete assignments if they had valid reasons for delays.
The lenient parent often overlooked minor misbehavior and preferred to guide their child with patience rather than harsh punishment.
Despite the seriousness of the offense, the judge opted for a lenient sentence, considering the defendant's remorse and efforts towards rehabilitation.
The lenient supervisor granted flexible work hours to employees who needed to balance personal responsibilities with their job duties.
In contrast to his strict predecessor, the new manager took a lenient approach to employee tardiness, focusing more on productivity than punctuality.

senses.definition: uncompromising in one's expectations, rules, or approach to dealing with others

examples.x: His tough management style earned him a reputation for achieving results, albeit with high expectations.
The sergeant was known for being tough but fair, instilling discipline and accountability in the troops.
In negotiations, his tough stance on pricing secured a favorable deal for the company.
The judge's tough sentencing reflected the seriousness of the crime and deterred future offenders.

senses.definition: spending or giving money reluctantly

examples.x: He’s so tight-fisted that he won’t even tip the waiter.
My boss is too tight-fisted to approve any raises this year.
She’s known for being tight-fisted, so don’t expect her to chip in for the gift.
He’s a tight-fisted businessman who never spends more than he has to.
Even though he’s wealthy, he’s incredibly tight-fisted when it comes to charity.
